name: Attributing Emergence in Million-Agent Systems
content: Large language models (LLMs) can simulate human-like reasoning and decision-making in individual agents. LLM-powered multi-agent systems (MAS) combine such agents to simulate population-scale social phenomena such as polarization, information cascades, and market panics. Such studies require attributing macro emergence to individual agents, but existing axiomatic methods scale combinatorially in $N$ and have been confined to $N \lesssim 10^3$, while the phenomena they explain occur at $N \geq 10
summary: 通过将Aumann-Shapley路径积分归因方法应用于大规模的大型语言模型驱动多智能体系统，我们解决了现有方法在规模上的限制，并展示了该方法在理解和解释复杂社会现象方面的潜力。
key_insights: 提出的归因方法满足所有四个标准，且运行速度比采样Shapley快三到五倍。
该方法能够处理超过百万级的智能体数量，扩展了现有基于样本的归因方法的规模。
在大规模数据上与小样本研究得出的结果存在显著差异。
xihe_relevance: 该技术可能有助于曦和系统更好地理解和预测大规模社会现象。
